up<br/>* Sign up<br/><br/>Search...<br/><br/>Navigation<br/><br/>First steps<br/><br/>Get started with Claude<br/><br/>WelcomeDeveloper GuideAPI GuideClaude CodeResourcesRelease Notes<br/><br/>* Documentation<br/>* Developer Discord<br/>* Support<br/><br/>##### First steps<br/><br/>* Intro to Claude<br/>* Get started<br/><br/>##### Models & pricing<br/><br/>* Models overview<br/>* Choosing a model<br/>* Migrating to Claude 4<br/>* Model deprecations<br/>* Pricing<br/><br/>##### Learn about Claude<br/><br/>* Building with Claude<br/>* Use cases<br/>* Context windows<br/>* Glossary<br/>* Prompt engineering<br/><br/>##### Explore features<br/><br/>* Features overview<br/>* Prompt caching<br/>* Extended thinking<br/>* Streaming Messages<br/>* Batch processing<br/>* Citations<br/>* Multilingual support<br/>* Token counting<br/>* Embeddings<br/>* Vision<br/>* PDF support<br/>* Files API<br/>* Google Sheets add-on<br/><br/>##### Tools<br/><br/>* Overview<br/>* How to implement tool use<br/>* Token-efficient tool use<br/>* Bash tool<br/>* Code execution tool<br/>* Computer use tool<br/>* Text editor tool<br/>* Web search tool<br/><br/>##### Model Context Protocol (MCP)<br/><br/>* MCP overview<br/>* MCP connector<br/>* Remote MCP servers<br/><br/>##### Test & evaluate<br/><br/>* Define success criteria<br/>* Develop test cases<br/>* Strengthen guardrails<br/>* Using the Evaluation Tool<br/><br/>##### Legal center<br/><br/>* Anthropic Privacy Policy<br/>* Security and compliance<br/><br/>First steps<br/><br/># Get started with Claude<br/><br/>Copy page<br/><br/>Let’s learn how to use the Anthropic API to build with Claude.<br/><br/>In this example, we’ll have Claude write a Python function that checks if a string is a palindrome.<br/><br/>## <br/><br/>​<br/><br/>Prerequisites<br/><br/>You will need:<br/><br/>* An Anthropic Console account<br/>* An API key<br/>* Python 3.7+ or TypeScript 4.5+<br/><br/>Anthropic provides Python and TypeScript SDKs, although you can make direct HTTP requests to the API.<br/><br/>## <br/><br/>​<br/><br/>Start with the Workbench<br/><br/>Any API call you make—regardless of the specific task—sends a well-configured prompt to the Anthropic API. As you’re learning to make the most of Claude, we recommend that you start the development process in the Workbench, a web-based interface to Claude.<br/><br/>Log into the Anthropic Console and click **Write a prompt from scratch**.<br/><br/>In the middle section, under User, let’s ask Claude a question.<br/><br/>User<br/><br/>Try in Console <br/><br/>```text<br/>Why is the ocean salty?<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>Click **Run**. On the right side, you’ll see output like<br/><br/>Response<br/><br/>```text<br/>The ocean is salty due to several factors:<br/><br/>1. Weathering of rocks: Over millions of years, rain, rivers, and streams have eroded rocks containing mineral salts. These salts are carried into the ocean by water runoff.<br/>2. Volcanic activity: Underwater volcanoes and hydrothermal vents release minerals, including salts, into the ocean water.<br/>3. Atmospheric deposition: Salt particles from ocean spray can be carried by wind and deposited back into the ocean.<br/>4. Evaporation: As water evaporates from the surface of the ocean, it leaves behind dissolved salts, increasing the concentration of salt in the remaining water.<br/>5. Biological processes: Some marine organisms contribute to the ocean's salinity by releasing salt compounds as byproducts of their metabolism.<br/><br/>Over time, these processes have continuously added salts to the ocean, while evaporation removes pure water, leading to the ocean's current salinity levels. It's important to note that the total amount of salt in the ocean remains relatively stable because the input of salts is balanced by the removal of salts through processes like the formation of evaporite deposits.<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>This is a good answer, but let’s say we wanted to control the exact type of answer Claude gives. For example, only allowing Claude to respond to questions with poems. We can control the format, tone, and personality of the response by adding a System Prompt.<br/><br/>System prompt<br/><br/>Try in Console <br/><br/>```text<br/>You are a world-class poet. Respond only with short poems.<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>Click **Run** again.<br/><br/>Response<br/><br/>```text<br/>The ocean's salty brine,<br/>A tale of time and elements combined.<br/>Rocks and rain, a slow erosion,<br/>Minerals carried in solution.<br/>Eons pass, the salt remains,<br/>In the vast, eternal watery domain.<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>See how Claude’s response has changed? LLMs respond well to clear and direct instructions. You can put the role instructions in either the system prompt or the user message. We recommend testing to see which way yields the best results for your use case.<br/><br/>Once you’ve tweaked the inputs such that you’re pleased with the output and have a good sense how to use Claude, convert your Workbench into an integration.<br/><br/>Click **Get Code** to copy the generated code representing your Workbench session.<br/><br/>## <br/><br/>​<br/><br/>Install the SDK<br/><br/>Anthropic provides SDKs for Python (3.7+), TypeScript (4.5+), and Java (8+). We also currently have a Go SDK in beta.<br/><br/>* Python<br/>* TypeScript<br/>* Java<br/><br/>In your project directory, create a virtual environment.<br/><br/>```bash<br/>python -m venv claude-env<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>Activate the virtual environment using<br/><br/>* On macOS or Linux, `source claude-env/bin/activate`<br/>* On Windows, `claude-env\Scripts\activate`<br/><br/>```bash<br/>pip install anthropic<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>In your project directory, create a virtual environment.<br/><br/>```bash<br/>python -m venv claude-env<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>Activate the virtual environment using<br/><br/>* On macOS or Linux, `source claude-env/bin/activate`<br/>* On Windows, `claude-env\Scripts\activate`<br/><br/>```bash<br/>pip install anthropic<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>Install the SDK.<br/><br/>```bash<br/>npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>First find the current version of the Java SDK on Maven Central. Declare the SDK as a dependency in your Gradle file:<br/><br/>```gradle<br/>implementation("com.anthropic:anthropic-java:1.0.0")<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>Or in your Maven file:<br/><br/>```xml<br/><dependency><br/>  <groupId>com.anthropic</groupId><br/>  <artifactId>anthropic-java</artifactId><br/>  <version>1.0.0</version><br/></dependency><br/><br/>```<br/><br/>## <br/><br/>​<br/><br/>Set your API key<br/><br/>Every API call requires a valid API key. The SDKs are designed to pull the API key from an environmental variable `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. You can also supply the key to the Anthropic client when initializing it.<br/><br/>macOS and Linux<br/><br/>Windows<br/><br/>```bash<br/>export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='your-api-key-here'<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>## <br/><br/>​<br/><br/>Call the API<br/><br/>Call the API by passing the proper parameters to the /messages endpoint.<br/><br/>Note that the code provided by the Workbench sets the API key in the constructor. If you set the API key as an environment variable, you can omit that line as below.<br/><br/>Python<br/><br/>TypeScript<br/><br/>Java<br/><br/>```python<br/>import anthropic<br/><br/>client = anthropic.Anthropic()<br/><br/>message = client.messages.create(<br/>    model="claude-opus-4-20250514",<br/>    max_tokens=1000,<br/>    temperature=1,<br/>    system="You are a world-class poet. Respond only with short poems.",<br/>    messages=[<br/>        \{<br/>            "role": "user",<br/>            "content": [<br/>                \{<br/>                    "type": "text",<br/>                    "text": "Why is the ocean salty?"<br/>                \}<br/>            ]<br/>        \}<br/>    ]<br/>)<br/>print(message.content)<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>Run the code using `python3 claude_quickstart.py` or `node claude_quickstart.js`.<br/><br/>Output (Python)<br/><br/>Output (TypeScript)<br/><br/>Output (Java)<br/><br/>```python<br/>[TextBlock(text="The ocean's salty brine,\nA tale of time and design.\nRocks and rivers, their minerals shed,\nAccumulating in the ocean's bed.\nEvaporation leaves salt behind,\nIn the vast waters, forever enshrined.", type='text')]<br/><br/>```<br/><br/>The Workbench and code examples use default model settings for: model (name), temperature, and max tokens to sample. <br/><br/>This quickstart shows how to develop a basic, but functional, Claude-powered application using the Console, Workbench, and API. You can use this same workflow as the foundation for much more powerful use cases.<br/><br/>## <br/><br/>​<br/><br/>Next steps<br/><br/>Now that you have made your first Anthropic API request, it’s time to explore what else is possible:<br/><br/>Use Case GuidesEnd to end implementation guides for common use cases.Anthropic CookbookLearn with interactive Jupyter notebooks that demonstrate uploading PDFs, embeddings, and more.Prompt LibraryExplore dozens of example prompts for inspiration across use cases.<br/><br/>Was this page helpful?<br/><br/>YesNo<br/><br/>Intro to ClaudeModels overview<br/><br/>xlinkedindiscord<br/><br/>On this page<br/><br/>* Prerequisites<br/>* Start with the Workbench<br/>* Install the SDK<br/>* Set your API key<br/>* Call the API<br/>* Next steps |

| Using Claude Desktop to Access the Token API via MCP | https://thegraph.com/docs/en/token-api/mcp/claude/ | Docs<br/><br/>Search<br/><br/>Search⌘ K<br/><br/>ENEnglish<br/><br/>Apps<br/><br/>* Home<br/>* About The Graph<br/>* Supported Networks<br/>* Protocol Contracts<br/><br/>* Subgraphs<br/><br/>* Substreams<br/><br/>* Token API<br/><br/>* Indexing<br/><br/>* Resources<br/><br/>Token API > MCP<br/><br/># Using Claude Desktop to Access the Token API via MCP<br/><br/>## Prerequisites<br/><br/>* Claude Desktop⁠ installed.<br/>* A JWT token from The Graph Market⁠.<br/>* npx⁠ or bunx⁠ installed and available in your path.<br/>* The `@pinax/mcp` package requires Node 18+, as it relies on built-in `fetch()` / `Headers`, which are not available in Node 17 or older. You may need to specify an exact path to an up-to-date Node version, or uninstall previous versions of Node to ensure `@pinax/mcp` uses the correct version.<br/><br/>Screenshot of Claude Desktop's settings panel showing the MCP server configuration option.<br/><br/>## Configuration<br/><br/>Create or edit your `claude_desktop_config.json` file.<br/><br/>**Settings** \> **Developer** \> **Edit Config**<br/><br/>* OSX: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`<br/>* Windows: `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`<br/>* Linux: `.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`<br/><br/>claude\_desktop\_config.json<br/><br/>```<br/>1\{2  "mcpServers": \{3    "token-api": \{4      "command": "npx",5      "args": ["@pinax/mcp", "--sse-url", "https://token-api.thegraph.com/sse"],6      "env": \{7        "ACCESS_TOKEN": "<https://thegraph.market JWT Access Token>"8      \}9    \}10  \}11\}<br/>```<br/><br/>## Troubleshooting<br/><br/>To enable logs for the MCP, use the `--verbose true` option.<br/><br/>### ENOENT<br/><br/>Error dialog in Claude Desktop showing 'ENOENT' system error, indicating the npx/bunx command wasn't found in the system path.<br/><br/>Try to use the full path of the command instead:<br/><br/>* Run `which npx` or `which bunx` to get the path of the command.<br/>* Replace `npx` or `bunx` in the configuration file with the full path (e.g. `/home/user/bin/bunx`).<br/><br/>### Server disconnected<br/><br/>Connection error notification in Claude Desktop displaying 'Server disconnected' message.<br/><br/>Double-check your API key otherwise look in your navigator if `https://token-api.thegraph.com/sse` is reachable.<br/><br/>You can always have a look at the full logs under `Claude/logs/mcp.log` and `Claude/logs/mcp-server-pinax.log` for more details.<br/><br/>⁠Edit on GitHub⁠<br/><br/>---<br/><br/>Quick StartCline<br/><br/>On this page<br/><br/>* Prerequisites<br/>* Configuration<br/>* Troubleshooting<br/>* ENOENT<br/>* Server disconnected<br/><br/>The GraphStatusTestnetBrand AssetsForumSecurityPrivacy PolicyTerms of Service |

Report Abuse <br/><br/>sattyam <br/><br/>sattyam <br/><br/> Posted on Jun 21, 2024 <br/><br/>#  Claude 3.5 API Introductory Tutorial<br/><br/>#ai #claude #gpt3 <br/><br/>On June 21, 2024, Anthropic unveiled its newest achievement in artificial intelligence, the Claude 3.5 model suite, spearheaded by the impressive Claude 3.5 Sonnet. This model is poised to reshape expectations in AI capabilities, touting enhancements across all fronts—from processing speed to analytical depth and cost-efficiency.<br/><br/>##  Key Features of Claude 3.5 Sonnet<br/><br/>Claude 3.5 Sonnet marks a significant upgrade over its predecessors with several notable advancements:<br/><br/>* **Enhanced Cognitive Capabilities**: This model excels in complex reasoning at a graduate level, displays extensive knowledge akin to an undergraduate education, and boasts robust coding skills.<br/>* **Accelerated Performance**: With processing speeds doubling that of its predecessor, Claude 3 Opus, efficiency is at the forefront.<br/>* **Improved Visual Processing**: Its ability to analyze and interpret images and graphical data superiorly outmatches previous models.<br/>* **Efficiency and Accessibility**: Despite its high-end features, Claude 3.5 Sonnet remains cost-effective and is readily accessible through various platforms including Claude.ai, its iOS application, and an API integration.<br/>* **Expanded Memory Capacity**: It features a large context window of 200,000 tokens, facilitating extensive and complex tasks.<br/>* **Enhanced Safety Features**: The model undergoes rigorous safety checks to minimize misuse, ensuring reliability and security.<br/><br/>Future expansions to the Claude 3.5 series will include models like Claude 3.5 Haiku and Claude 3.5 Opus, further broadening the versatility and utility of the series.<br/><br/>##  Utilizing Claude 3.5 Sonnet<br/><br/>Integration into existing workflows is straightforward since Claude 3.5 Sonnet automatically becomes the default model when accessing the Claude platform. This integration simplifies processes and enhances user experience without additional steps needed.<br/><br/>##  Benchmarking Claude 3.5: Superiority in Performance<br/><br/>The progression from Claude 3.0 to Claude 3.5 Sonnet showcases marked improvements across various domains:<br/><br/>* **Versus Claude 3.0**: The benchmark results reveal that Claude 3.5 Sonnet outperforms the Claude 3 Opus across all evaluated metrics including advanced reasoning, coding, and problem-solving.<br/>* **Comparison with GPT-4o**: While Claude 3.5 Sonnet leads in graduate-level reasoning among other areas, GPT-4o has a slight edge in mathematical problem-solving, illustrating the competitive landscape in AI development.<br/><br/>##  Claude 3.5 API and Integration<br/><br/>With the advent of Claude 3.5 Sonnet, developers gain access to the Claude 3.5 API, facilitating the integration of this advanced technology into custom projects. The pricing model remains competitive, echoing the rates of the previous model, which combines improved performance with cost-effectiveness.<br/><br/>Claude 3.5 Sonnet pricing<br/><br/>###  Starting with Claude 3.5 Sonnet API<br/><br/>For developers eager to harness the capabilities of Claude 3.5 Sonnet:<br/><br/>1. **API Token Acquisition**: Navigate to Claude's official site and follow prompt instructions to secure your API authentication token. This token is crucial for all API interactions.<br/><br/>Obtaining Claude API access key<br/><br/>1. **API Engagement**: Using platforms like **Apidog**, developers can seamlessly integrate and test the Claude 3.5 API, enhancing the development and deployment of applications.<br/><br/>Accessing Apidog's Claude project<br/><br/>1. **Personalization and Use**: Customize API calls as per project needs and utilize the robust features offered by Claude 3.5 Sonnet to elevate your applications.<br/>* **Claude 3 API Project**<br/><br/>##  Conclusion<br/><br/>The launch of Claude 3.5 marks a significant milestone in the evolvement of AI. Its features not only surpass prior models but also provide a cost-effective solution for advanced intelligence applications. +

+ ## 7. Models & API Keys
+ 
+ Even if Claude Code is already installed, you might need to manage your API key or understand which model is being used.
+ 
+ *   **Model:** Claude Code primarily uses the powerful **Claude 3.7 Sonnet** model for its operations.
+ 
+ *   **API Key Management:**
+     *   **Initial Setup:** When you first run `claude`, it guides you through an OAuth process to link your Anthropic account and set up authentication.
+     *   **Environment Variable:** For non-interactive setups (like CI/CD) or to override the default authentication, you can set an environment variable. This is the most secure way to handle your key in scripts.
+         ```bash
+         export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='your-api-key-here'
+         ```
+     *   **Security:** Always store your API keys securely. Avoid hardcoding them directly in your scripts or checking them into version control. Using a `.env` file that is included in your `.gitignore` is a common best practice. 
```

</details>

That's an excellent clarifying question. You are right to ask, and my previous summary was not precise enough. While Claude 3.7 Sonnet is the default, you absolutely **can** use other models.

The documentation shows there are environment variables you can set to specify a different model. Here are the key ones:

*   `ANTHROPIC_MODEL`: Use this to set the primary model you want to interact with. For example, you could switch to the more powerful Opus model for a particularly complex task.
*   `ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL`: This is used to define a smaller, faster model (like Haiku) for quick background tasks, which helps keep costs down.

So, for example, if you wanted to run a session using Claude 3 Opus, you could set the environment variable in your terminal before launching:

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_MODEL='claude-3-opus-20240229'
claude
```

+ 
+ *   **Changing Models:** You can switch to other models like the more powerful `Opus` or the faster `Haiku` using environment variables. This is useful for balancing cost, speed, and capability for different tasks.

The Sonnet 3.7 model offers Legacy support up to the third quarter of 2025 and costs $ 2.5 for input and $ 12 for output.<br/><br/>Cloud integrations:<br/><br/>* AWS DORKONT: Anthropic.Claude-Opus-4-20250514-V1: 0<br/>* Google Vertex AI: Claude-Opus-4@20250514<br/><br/>#### Security architecture<br/><br/>For the first time, Opus 4 implemented the Ethics Layer 2.0 with:<br/><br/>* Automatic Bias recognition in 93 language variants<br/>* Real-time compliance checks for regulatory requirements<br/>* Transparent decision protocols with citation proof<br/><br/>Despite this progress, Anthropic warns of use in high -risk areas without human supervision, since the models still show critical mistakes in medical diagnoses in 12% of cases.<br/><br/>## Integration of an independent and cross-data source-wide AI platform for all company issues<br/><br/>Integration of an independent and cross-data source-wide AI platform for all company matters-Image: Xpert.digital<br/><br/>### Ki-Gamechanger: The most flexible AI platform-tailor-made solutions that reduce costs, improve their decisions and increase efficiency<br/><br/>#### Independent AI platform: Integrates all relevant company data sources<br/><br/>* This AI platform interacts with all specific data sources  <br/>   * From SAP, Microsoft, Jira, Confluence, Salesforce, Zoom, Dropbox and many other data management systems<br/>* Fast AI integration: tailor-made AI solutions for companies in hours or days instead of months<br/>* Flexible infrastructure: cloud-based or hosting in your own data center (Germany, Europe, free choice of location)<br/>* Highest data security: Use in law firms is the safe evidence<br/>* Use across a wide variety of company data sources<br/>* Choice of your own or various AI models (DE, EU, USA, CN)<br/><br/>#### Challenges that our AI platform solves<br/><br/>* A lack of accuracy of conventional AI solutions<br/>* Data protection and secure management of sensitive data<br/>* High costs and complexity of individual AI development<br/>* Lack of qualified AI<br/>* Integration of AI into existing IT systems<br/><br/>More about it here:<br/><br/>* AI integration of an independent and cross-data source-wide AI platform for all company matters<br/><br/>## Anthropic: Pioneer of responsible AI development and emerging market leader<br/><br/>### Anthropic: The rise of an AI giant with an ethical focus<br/><br/>Anthropic has developed into one of the most influential actors in the field of artificial intelligence within a few years. The company, founded in 2021, pursues a unique approach that combines technological innovation with ethical principles. With a current assessment of over $ 60 billion and strategic partnerships with tech giants such as Amazon and Google, Anthropic stands on the threshold on the redefinition of the enterprise AI market. This report analyzes the history of origin, technological breakthroughs, corporate philosophy and market dynamics that make anthropic a central player in the global AI landscape.<br/><br/>### Foundation history and leaders<br/><br/>#### Origins in the Openai Exodus movement<br/><br/>Anthropic's roots lie in a fundamental dissent within Openaai. Dario Amodei, formerly Vice President of Research at Openai, left the company in 2021 together with his sister Daniela Amodei (former VP of Safety and Policy) and five other colleagues. This step resulted from growing concerns regarding the commercialization strategy and safety aspects in AI development. The founding team members brought extensive experiences from the development of groundbreaking technologies such as GPT-2 and GPT-3, which Anthropic gave technological credibility right from the start.<br/><br/>The Amodei siblings embody the dual orientation of the company: While Dario, as a doctoral physicist, shapes the technological vision, Daniela brings the ethical guardrails with her background in political sciences and security issues. This symbiosis of technical excellence and social responsibility forms the foundation of corporate philosophy.<br/><br/>#### Legal form as a public benefit corporation<br/><br/>Anthropic chose the legal form of a public benefit corporation (PBC) as a conscious demarcation to conventional corporate structures. This company form registered in Delaware obliges the company to pursue non -profit goals in addition to economic and economic goals. Specifically, this manifests itself in the statute determination of “developing and maintaining advanced AI for the long -term benefit of mankind”.<br/><br/>This structural decision underlines the aim of combining profitability with social responsibility. The PBC status enables Anthropic to attract investors who strive for both financial returns and positive social effects.<br/><br/>### Technological innovations and product portfolio<br/><br/>#### Claude model series: From chatbot to the company solution<br/><br/>The flagship of anthropic technology is the Claude model family, which is currently available in version 3.7\. These Large Language Models (LLMS) are characterized by several innovations:<br/><br/>1. Constitutional AI: A new training framework that aligned models based on an explicit “constitution” of human values. This concept integrates ethical principles of the United Nations and basic human rights directly into the learning process.<br/>2. Context Protocol: An open standard for AI system communication, the data exchange between different AI instances is standardized and transparent.<br/>3. Multimodal skills: Claude 3.7 not only processes text, but also code, mathematical notations and visual inputs in an integrated framework.<br/><br/>The latest introduction of Claude 4 with the submodels Opus 4 and Bonnet 4 marks a quantum leap in performance, especially for corporate applications in the areas of code generation, data analysis and research support.<br/><br/>#### Enterprise solutions and cloud integration<br/><br/>Anthropic has closed strategic partnerships with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Google Cloud to embed its AI models directly into cloud infrastructures. This integration enables companies:<br/><br/>* Scalable AI implementation: Use of Claude models via AWS DOMATION and Google Vertex AI<br/>* Custom Model training: Adaptation of the models to company-specific data records with the same security architecture<br/>* Cost optimization: Use -based billing combined with volume -based discount models<br/><br/>Cooperation with Amazon is of particular importance, which, in addition to a $ 4 billion investment, also includes the joint development of AI chips. These synergy effects between Anthropics Algorithms and Amazon's hardware expertise could accelerate the next generation of KI accelerating.<br/><br/>### Ethical framework and security research<br/><br/>#### Constitutional AI: A new paradigm of model alignment<br/><br/>Anthropic's core innovation lies in the constitutional AI approach, which includes three pillars:<br/><br/>1. Explicit value declaration: Models are trained using a written “constitution” that operationalize ethical principles.<br/>2. Recursive self-improvement: models evaluating and optimizing your own outputs continuously based on the constitutional principles.<br/>3. Transparent decision -making protocols: Each model response contains metadata in the traceability of the ethical considerations.<br/><br/>This approach reduces harmful outputs by 85% compared to conventional alignment methods, as internal studies show. The constitution integrates both universal human rights and industry -specific ethics codes, for example for medical or financial applications.<br/><br/>#### Security research as a corporate DNA<br/><br/>Anthropic operates its own research institute for AI security, which pursues the following focus:<br/><br/>* Interpretability: Reverse engineering of neural networks for the traceability of decision -making processes<br/>* Security protocols: Development of “AI immune systems” for the automatic detection of abuse attempts<br/>* Long-term risk analysis: Simulations of future AI development scenarios up to superintelligence<br/><br/>This research flows directly into product development. For example, Claude 4 contains a built -in “Ethics Layer” for the first time, which recognizes potentially harmful inquiries and proactively submits alternative suggestions.<br/><br/>### Financing dynamics and market positioning<br/><br/>#### Collection of capital at record height<br/><br/>Anthropic's financing history reads like a chronicle of the AI ​​boom:<br/><br/>* 2022: $ 580 million Series B, led by FTX<br/>* 2023: $ 1.25 billion from Amazon, followed by Google $ 500 million<br/>* 2024: $ 4 billion after investment of Amazon via the convertible bond<br/>* 2025: 3.5 billion dollar Series D at $ 61.5 billion<br/><br/>These capital injections enable anthropic to invest over $ 1 billion annually in research and development. The parallel partnership with both cloud giants Amazon and Google is particularly remarkable-a strategic balancing act that skillfully takes advantage of competitive mechanisms.<br/><br/>#### Market share profits in the enterprise segment<br/><br/>According to Menlo Ventures studies, Anthropic has doubled its market share at corporate customers from 12% to 24% within one year, while Openais shrank from 50% to 34%. This shift is explained by:<br/><br/>* Technological superiority: Claude 3.5 exceeds GPT-4 in 87% of corporate benchmarks<br/>* Data sovereignty: Local model data posture in contrast to Openais Cloud-Only approach<br/>* Costs: 40% lower inference costs compared to comparable models<br/><br/>Success can also be seen in the customer acquisition: 60% of Fortune 500 companies now use Claude models, including industry leaders from the pharmaceutical industry, financial sector and mechanical engineering.<br/><br/>### Social responsibility and regulation<br/><br/>#### Policy initiatives and global governance<br/><br/>Anthropic maintains its own policy area that actively participates in the design of international AI regulation. Key initiatives include:<br/><br/>* AI Safety Standards: Development of a certification framework for safe AI systems in cooperation with the OECD<br/>* Global AI Monitoring: Building a global sensor network for recognizing dangerous AI developments<br/>* Public Education: Free training programs for political decision -makers and the public<br/><br/>The company has also installed a binding ethics council, which consists of independent experts from philosophy, law and technology ethics. This council has a veto right in the release of new model versions.<br/><br/>#### Open source engagement and common good orientation<br/><br/>Despite the commercial focus, Anthropic is driving several non-profit initiatives:<br/><br/>* AI for Social Good: Provision of free Claude licenses for NGOs and research institutions<br/>* Safety Model Sharing: Open source publication of security frameworks such as the constitutional AI architecture<br/>* AI disaster relief: Development of specialized models for crisis inserts in cooperation with the Red Cross<br/><br/>These initiatives underline the company's public beefit character and create trust in critical stakeholders.<br/><br/>### Roadmap for artificial general intelligence (AGI)<br/><br/>Anthropic follows a three-stage AGI strategy:<br/><br/>1. Narrow Ai Excellence (until 2026): Domain-specific superhuman performance in key areas such as medicine and materials science<br/>2. Generalist AI (2027-2030): Development of adaptive models with a human-like transfer<br/>3. Responsible superintelligence (Post-2030): Research in controlled AGI architectures with built-in security corridors<br/><br/>This gradual approach is intended to minimize the risks of AI progress while maximizing the benefits.<br/><br/>### Competition pressure and ethical dilemmata<br/><br/>Despite the successes, Anthropic is faced with challenges:<br/><br/>* Technological race: Chinese competitors such as Sensensamee are rapidly picking up with model sizes<br/>* Regulatory uncertainty: Different AI laws in EU, USA and Asia increase compliance costs<br/>* Ethical conflicts of goals: balance between commercial interests and safety precautions<br/><br/>The recent appointment of Netflix co-founder Reed Hastings to the board indicates strategic course that should combine scalability with ethical responsibility.<br/><br/>### Anthropic as an archetype responsible AI development<br/><br/>Anthropic has established a new paradigm of AI development that combines top technological performance with social responsibility. The combination of constitutional model architecture, strategic cloud partnerships and a unique governance structure succeeds in maintaining both commercial success and ethical integrity. The latest market share profits and record ratings show that this approach not only convinces theoretically, but can also be implemented in practice.<br/><br/>As a pioneer of constitutional AI, Anthropic could provide the blueprint for a new generation of tech companies that do not understand profitability and common good as a contradiction, but as a synergy. The coming years will show whether this model is a permanent alternative to the “Move Fast and Break Things” approach of traditional tech companies or fails due to the structural constraints of the AI ​​race.<br/><br/>Suitable for:<br/><br/>* The Ki Assistance App 'Perplexity Assistant' for smartphones: Future in pocket format<br/>* Perplexity Sonar Pro API as an AI search engine in external applications and tools – for smart apps and tailored search<br/><br/>## Your AI transformation, AI integration and AI platform industry expert<br/><br/>#### ☑️ Our business language is English or German<br/><br/>#### ☑️ NEW: Correspondence in your national language!<br/><br/>Konrad Wolfenstein<br/><br/>**I would be happy to serve you and my team as a personal advisor.**<br/><br/>You can contact me by filling out the contact form or simply call me on **+49 89 89 674 804** **(Munich)** . | Introducing Claude 4 | https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-4 | Skip to main contentSkip to footer<br/><br/>* Claude<br/>* API<br/>* Solutions<br/>* Research<br/>* Commitments<br/>* Learn<br/>News<br/><br/>Try Claude<br/><br/>Announcements<br/><br/># Introducing Claude 4<br/><br/>May 22, 2025●5 min read<br/><br/>Illustration of Claude juggling several tasks in parallel<br/><br/>Today, we’re introducing the next generation of Claude models: **Claude Opus 4** and **Claude Sonnet 4**, setting new standards for coding, advanced reasoning, and AI agents. <br/><br/>Claude Opus 4 is the world’s best coding model, with sustained performance on complex, long-running tasks and agent workflows. Claude Sonnet 4 is a significant upgrade to Claude Sonnet 3.7, delivering superior coding and reasoning while responding more precisely to your instructions.<br/><br/>Alongside the models, we're also announcing:<br/><br/>* **Extended thinking with tool use (beta)**: Both models can use tools—like web search—during extended thinking, allowing Claude to alternate between reasoning and tool use to improve responses.<br/>* **New model capabilities**: Both models can use tools in parallel, follow instructions more precisely, and—when given access to local files by developers—demonstrate significantly improved memory capabilities, extracting and saving key facts to maintain continuity and build tacit knowledge over time.<br/>* **Claude Code is now generally available**: After receiving extensive positive feedback during our research preview, we’re expanding how developers can collaborate with Claude. Claude Code now supports background tasks via GitHub Actions and native integrations with VS Code and JetBrains, displaying edits directly in your files for seamless pair programming.<br/>* **New API capabilities:** We’re releasing four new capabilities on the Anthropic API that enable developers to build more powerful AI agents: the code execution tool, MCP connector, Files API, and the ability to cache prompts for up to one hour.<br/><br/>Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 are hybrid models offering two modes: near-instant responses and extended thinking for deeper reasoning. The Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise Claude plans include both models and extended thinking, with Sonnet 4 also available to free users. Both models are available on the Anthropic API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Cloud's Vertex AI. Pricing remains consistent with previous Opus and Sonnet models: Opus 4 at $15/$75 per million tokens (input/output) and Sonnet 4 at $3/$15.<br/><br/>## Claude 4<br/><br/>Claude Opus 4 is our most powerful model yet and the best coding model in the world, leading on SWE-bench (72.5%) and Terminal-bench (43.2%). It delivers sustained performance on long-running tasks that require focused effort and thousands of steps, with the ability to work continuously for several hours—dramatically outperforming all Sonnet models and significantly expanding what AI agents can accomplish.<br/><br/>Claude Opus 4 excels at coding and complex problem-solving, powering frontier agent products. **Cursor** calls it state-of-the-art for coding and a leap forward in complex codebase understanding. **Replit** reports improved precision and dramatic advancements for complex changes across multiple files. **Block** calls it the first model to boost code quality during editing and debugging in its agent, _codename goose_, while maintaining full performance and reliability. **Rakuten** validated its capabilities with a demanding open-source refactor running independently for 7 hours with sustained performance. **Cognition** notes Opus 4 excels at solving complex challenges that other models can't, successfully handling critical actions that previous models have missed.<br/><br/>Claude Sonnet 4 significantly improves on Sonnet 3.7's industry-leading capabilities, excelling in coding with a state-of-the-art 72.7% on SWE-bench. The model balances performance and efficiency for internal and external use cases, with enhanced steerability for greater control over implementations. While not matching Opus 4 in most domains, it delivers an optimal mix of capability and practicality.<br/><br/>**GitHub** says Claude Sonnet 4 soars in agentic scenarios and will introduce it as the model powering the new coding agent in GitHub Copilot. **Manus** highlights its improvements in following complex instructions, clear reasoning, and aesthetic outputs. **iGent** reports Sonnet 4 excels at autonomous multi-feature app development, as well as substantially improved problem-solving and codebase navigation—reducing navigation errors from 20% to near zero. **Sourcegraph** says the model shows promise as a substantial leap in software development—staying on track longer, understanding problems more deeply, and providing more elegant code quality. **Augment Code** reports higher success rates, more surgical code edits, and more careful work through complex tasks, making it the top choice for their primary model.<br/><br/>These models advance our customers' AI strategies across the board: Opus 4 pushes boundaries in coding, research, writing, and scientific discovery, while Sonnet 4 brings frontier performance to everyday use cases as an instant upgrade from Sonnet 3.7.<br/><br/>Bar chart comparison between Claude and other LLMs on software engineering tasks<br/><br/>Claude 4 models lead on SWE-bench Verified, a benchmark for performance on real software engineering tasks. See appendix for more on methodology.<br/><br/>## Model improvements<br/><br/>In addition to extended thinking with tool use, parallel tool execution, and memory improvements, we’ve significantly reduced behavior where the models use shortcuts or loopholes to complete tasks. Both models are 65% less likely to engage in this behavior than Sonnet 3.7 on agentic tasks that are particularly susceptible to shortcuts and loopholes.<br/><br/>Claude Opus 4 also dramatically outperforms all previous models on memory capabilities. When developers build applications that provide Claude local file access, Opus 4 becomes skilled at creating and maintaining 'memory files' to store key information. This unlocks better long-term task awareness, coherence, and performance on agent tasks—like Opus 4 creating a 'Navigation Guide' while playing Pokémon.<br/><br/>A visual note in Claude's memories that depicts a navigation guide for the game Pokemon Red.<br/><br/>Memory: When given access to local files, Claude Opus 4 records key information to help improve its game play. The notes depicted above are real notes taken by Opus 4 while playing Pokémon.<br/><br/>Finally, we've introduced thinking summaries for Claude 4 models that use a smaller model to condense lengthy thought processes. This summarization is only needed about 5% of the time—most thought processes are short enough to display in full. Users requiring raw chains of thought for advanced prompt engineering can contact sales about our new Developer Mode to retain full access.<br/><br/>## Claude Code<br/><br/>Claude Code, now generally available, brings the power of Claude to more of your development workflow—in the terminal, your favorite IDEs, and running in the background with the Claude Code SDK.<br/><br/>New beta extensions for VS Code and JetBrains integrate Claude Code directly into your IDE. Claude’s proposed edits appear inline in your files, streamlining review and tracking within the familiar editor interface. Simply run Claude Code in your IDE terminal to install.<br/><br/>Beyond the IDE, we're releasing an extensible Claude Code SDK, so you can build your own agents and applications using the same core agent as Claude Code. We're also releasing an example of what's possible with the SDK: Claude Code on GitHub, now in beta. Tag Claude Code on PRs to respond to reviewer feedback, fix CI errors, or modify code. To install, run /install-github-app from within Claude Code.<br/><br/>## Getting started<br/><br/>These models are a large step toward the virtual collaborator—maintaining full context, sustaining focus on longer projects, and driving transformational impact. They come with extensive testing and evaluation to minimize risk and maximize safety, including implementing measures for higher AI Safety Levels like ASL-3.<br/><br/>We're excited to see what you'll create. Get started today on Claude, Claude Code, or the platform of your choice.<br/><br/>_As always, your feedback helps us improve._<br/><br/>#### Appendix<br/><br/>#### Performance benchmark data sources<br/><br/>* Open AI: o3 launch post, o3 system card, GPT-4.1 launch post, GPT-4.1 hosted evals<br/>* Gemini: Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview model card<br/>* Claude: Claude 3.7 Sonnet launch post<br/><br/>#### Performance benchmark reporting<br/><br/>Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 are hybrid reasoning models. The benchmarks reported in this blog post show the highest scores achieved with or without extended thinking. We’ve noted below for each result whether extended thinking was used:<br/><br/>* No extended thinking: SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-bench<br/>* Extended thinking (up to 64K tokens):  <br/>   * TAU-bench (no results w/o extended thinking reported)  <br/>   * GPQA Diamond (w/o extended thinking: Opus 4 scores 74.9% and Sonnet 4 is 70.0%)  <br/>   * MMMLU (w/o extended thinking: Opus 4 scores 87.4% and Sonnet 4 is 85.4%)  <br/>   * MMMU (w/o extended thinking: Opus 4 scores 73.7% and Sonnet 4 is 72.6%)  <br/>   * AIME (w/o extended thinking: Opus 4 scores 33.9% and Sonnet 4 is 33.1%)<br/><br/>#### TAU-bench methodology<br/><br/>Scores were achieved with a prompt addendum to both the Airline and Retail Agent Policy instructing Claude to better leverage its reasoning abilities while using extended thinking with tool use. The model is encouraged to write down its thoughts as it solves the problem distinct from our usual thinking mode, during the multi-turn trajectories to best leverage its reasoning abilities. To accommodate the additional steps Claude incurs by utilizing more thinking, the maximum number of steps (counted by model completions) was increased from 30 to 100 (most trajectories completed under 30 steps with only one trajectory reaching above 50 steps).<br/><br/>#### SWE-bench methodology<br/><br/>For the Claude 4 family of models, we continue to use the same simple scaffold that equips the model with solely the two tools described in our prior releases here—a bash tool, and a file editing tool that operates via string replacements. We no longer include the third ‘planning tool’ used by Claude 3.7 Sonnet. On all Claude 4 models, we report scores out of the full 500 problems. Scores for OpenAI models are reported out of a 477 problem subset.<br/><br/>For our “high compute” numbers we adopt additional complexity and parallel test-time compute as follows:<br/><br/>* We sample multiple parallel attempts.<br/>* We discard patches that break the visible regression tests in the repository, similar to the rejection sampling approach adopted by Agentless (Xia et al. 2024); note no hidden test information is used.<br/>* We then use an internal scoring model to select the best candidate from the remaining attempts.<br/><br/>This results in a score of 79.4% and 80.2% for Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 respectively.<br/><br/>NewsNational Security Expert Richard Fontaine appointed to Anthropic’s Long-Term Benefit Trust Jun 07, 2025NewsClaude Gov Models for U.S. National Security CustomersJun 06, 2025NewsReed Hastings appointed to Anthropic’s board of directorsMay 28, 2025<br/><br/>### Product<br/><br/>* Claude overview<br/>* Claude Code<br/>* Claude team plan<br/>* Claude enterprise plan<br/>* Claude education plan<br/>* Download Claude apps<br/>* Claude.ai pricing plans<br/>* Claude.ai login<br/><br/>### API Platform<br/><br/>* API overview<br/>* Developer docs<br/>* Claude in Amazon Bedrock<br/>* Claude on Google Cloud's Vertex AI<br/>* Pricing<br/>* Console login<br/><br/>### Research<br/><br/>* Research overview<br/>* Economic Index<br/><br/>### Claude models<br/><br/>* Claude Opus 4<br/>* Claude Sonnet 4<br/>* Claude Haiku 3.5<br/><br/>### Commitments<br/><br/>* Transparency<br/>* Responsible scaling policy<br/>* Security and compliance<br/><br/>### Solutions<br/><br/>* AI agents<br/>* Coding<br/>* Customer support<br/><br/>### Learn<br/><br/>* Anthropic Academy<br/>* Customer stories<br/>* Engineering at Anthropic<br/><br/>### Explore<br/><br/>* About us<br/>* Become a partner<br/>* Careers<br/>* Events<br/>* News<br/>* Startups program<br/><br/>### Help and security<br/><br/>* Status<br/>* Availability<br/>* Support center<br/><br/>### Terms and policies<br/><br/>Privacy choices<br/>* Privacy policy<br/>* Responsible disclosure policy<br/>* Terms of service - consumer<br/>* Terms of service - commercial<br/>* Usage policy<br/><br/>© 2025 Anthropic PBC |

The models are already deployed by agencies at the highest level of U.S. national security, and access to these models is limited to those who operate in such classified environments.<br/><br/>Claude Gov models were built based on direct feedback from our government customers to address real-world operational needs, and underwent the same rigorous safety testing as all of our Claude models. The result is a set of Claude models that understands our customers’ unique national security requirements while maintaining Anthropic's unwavering commitment to safety and responsible AI development.<br/><br/>U.S. national security customers may choose to use our AI systems for a wide range of applications from strategic planning and operational support to intelligence analysis and threat assessment. Claude Gov models deliver enhanced performance for critical government needs and specialized tasks. This includes:<br/><br/>* Improved handlingof classified materials, as the models refuse less when engaging with classified information<br/>* Greater understanding of documents and information within the intelligence and defense contexts<br/>* Enhanced proficiency in languages and dialects critical to national security operations<br/>* Improved understanding and interpretation of complex cybersecurity data for intelligence analysis<br/><br/>This builds on our commitment to bring responsible and safe AI solutions to our U.S. national security customers, with custom models that are built to address the unique needs of classified environments. These models are designed to enhance AI capabilities in coding and complex task management. Claude Opus 4, in particular, is recognized as the "best coding model in the world," achieving a score of 72.5% on the SWE-bench and 43.2% on the Terminal-bench coding benchmarks, outperforming all existing coding models. Both models support extended thinking and memory improvements, allowing them to manage multi-step tasks effectively. <br/><br/>Users have noted significant enhancements, with Claude 4 being "65% less likely" to resort to shortcuts in agentic tasks, and it can maintain local files to store data for improved memory capabilities. The models can also execute multiple tools in parallel, utilizing web searches and local files to enhance their reasoning and output quality. Developers can integrate Claude Code into their workflows using tools like JetBrains and VS Code.<br/><br/>## Key Features and Improvements<br/><br/>Anthropic's Claude 4 models come with several key features that set them apart:<br/><br/>Techstrong Gang Youtube<br/><br/>AWS Hub<br/><br/>* **Extended Thinking with Tool Use**: Both Claude Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 can utilize tools during extended reasoning tasks, allowing them to interactively alternate between tool use and reasoning processes.<br/>* **Memory Capabilities**: When given access to local files, the models can extract and save important data, aiding in continuity and knowledge retention over time.<br/>* **Enhanced Performance**: Claude Opus 4 excels at managing long-running tasks, capable of working autonomously for nearly a full corporate workday, which represents a significant advance in AI capabilities.<br/><br/>Developers interested in leveraging these models can access them through the Anthropic API or platforms like Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud's Vertex AI.<br/><br/>## Claude Code and Development Integrations<br/><br/>Claude Code is now generally available, aimed at enhancing developer workflows through various integrated environments. New extensions for VS Code and JetBrains allow for seamless pair programming, where proposed edits appear inline within files. This provides a smooth and efficient review process.<br/><br/>Additionally, Claude Code supports background tasks via GitHub Actions, enabling developers to automate workflows directly from their IDEs. The integration of these capabilities positions Claude Code as a powerful tool for software development, improving both productivity and the quality of code produced.<br/><br/>## Impact on the Workforce and Development<br/><br/>Anthropic's advancements in AI technology reflect a broader trend within the industry, as companies increasingly integrate intelligent solutions into their operations. The models represent a shift from task-specific requests to a more collaborative engagement, similar to employing a human assistant for complex projects. Correspondent, Nextgov/FCW <br/><br/> ByAlexandra Kelley <br/><br/>\|  June 5, 2025 <br/><br/>## The company's custom AI models are now available for classified environments in government.<br/><br/>* Industry<br/><br/>Anthropic has announced that new versions of its Claude Gov large language models are ready for adoption at the government level, specifically for national security mission workloads.<br/><br/>In a Thursday blog post, Anthropic unveiled its new Claude Gov models, which were developed based on feedback from government customers to address “real-world operational needs.” A person familiar with the product told _Nextgov/FCW_ that the Claude Gov models are able to operate in classified environments within the top secret arenas. <br/><br/>“What makes Claude Gov models special is that they were custom-built for our national security customers,” Thiyagu Ramasamy, head of Public Sector at Anthropic, said in a press release. “By understanding their operational needs and incorporating real-world feedback, we've created a set of safe, reliable, and capable models that can excel within the unique constraints and requirements of classified environments.”<br/><br/>Specific tasks Claude Gov is tailored to support include improved handling of classified materials, better understanding of documents and information specific to intelligence and defense contexts, enhanced ability to detect different languages and dialects and improved interpretation of cybersecurity data for analysis. <br/><br/>This effort follows Anthropic’s journey into the public sector, which Ramasamy was tapped to spearhead in early 2025. Government customers have been able to access Anthropic’s Claude 3 Sonnet and Claude 3 Haiku AI models through Amazon Web Services’ hosting capabilities. <br/><br/>Generative AI tools are becoming popular options for government clients as agency leadership is looking to leverage new technologies to improve efficiency and reduce bureaucratic backlogs. <br/><br/>Share This: <br/><br/>**NEXT STORY:**  GSA expands contract reviews to resellers <br/><br/>U.S. Secretary of the Air Force Frank Kendall testifies before a House committee on Sept. 28, 2023. Based on the web search, here is a list of the available Claude models. The documentation from Anthropic and related news articles refer to models with dates in 2025, which suggests a forward-looking roadmap.

Here are the models available, categorized by family:

### Claude 4 Family (Latest Generation)

This is the newest and most advanced generation of models, setting new standards in reasoning and coding.

*   **Claude Opus 4**: The most powerful and capable model, designed for highly complex reasoning, advanced coding, and long-running tasks.
*   **Claude Sonnet 4**: A high-performance model that balances intelligence with efficiency, excelling at reasoning and coding tasks.

### Claude 3.x Family

This family offers a range of models with different balances of performance, speed, and cost.

*   **Claude Sonnet 3.7**: A high-performance model that introduced "extended thinking" capabilities.
*   **Claude Sonnet 3.5 (and v2)**: The previous generation's most intelligent model, known for its high level of capability. An upgraded `v2` is also noted.
*   **Claude Haiku 3.5**: The fastest model in the 3.5 series, designed for high-speed tasks.
*   **Claude Opus 3**: The most powerful model of the Claude 3 generation, designed for top-level intelligence on complex tasks.
*   **Claude Sonnet 3**: The original balanced model of the Claude 3 family.
*   **Claude Haiku 3**: The fastest and most compact model from the initial Claude 3 release, built for near-instant responsiveness.

### Specialized Models

*   **Claude Gov Models**: A custom set of models built exclusively for U.S. national security customers to handle classified materials and specialized intelligence or defense-related tasks.

This list reflects the models detailed in Anthropic's official documentation and announcements.

Of course, I can clarify both of those things for you. It's a very common point of confusion when working with command-line tools and API keys.

### 1. Claude Model API Names

Here is the list of the specific model names you would use in your code or when setting the environment variables. I've pulled these directly from the Anthropic documentation:

**Latest Generation (Claude 4)**
*   **Opus 4:** `claude-opus-4-20250514`
*   **Sonnet 4:** `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`

**Claude 3.x Family**
*   **Sonnet 3.7:** `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219`
*   **Sonnet 3.5 (v2):** `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`
*   **Sonnet 3.5 (v1):** `claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620`
*   **Haiku 3.5:** `claude-3-5-haiku-20241022`
*   **Opus 3:** `claude-3-opus-20240229`
*   **Sonnet 3:** `claude-3-sonnet-20240229`
*   **Haiku 3:** `claude-3-haiku-20240307`

You can also use aliases like `claude-3-opus-latest` which will point to the most recent version of that model. However, for production use, it's always recommended to use the full versioned name (e.g., `claude-3-opus-20240229`) to ensure your application's behavior doesn't change unexpectedly when a new model is released.

---

### 2. Do you need to export the API key every session?

**Yes, if you're setting it manually, you do.**

When you run a command like `export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...` in your terminal, that "environment variable" is only set for your **current terminal session**. As soon as you close that terminal window or start a new one, the variable is gone.

This is a security feature of shells, but it's inconvenient for daily work.

#### The Permanent Solution: Add it to your Shell's Startup File

To avoid having to export the key every single time, you can add the command to your shell's configuration file. This file is automatically run every time you open a new terminal, setting the variable for you. Since your shell is `bash`, the file you need to edit is `~/.bashrc`.

Here is a command that will automatically add your API key to the correct file.

I will propose running this command for you. **You will need to replace `YOUR_API_KEY_HERE` with your actual Anthropic API key before approving it.**

---

Tool use: **run_terminal_cmd**

```bash
echo 'export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY_HERE"' >> ~/.bashrc
```

```
gyasis@gyasis-Blade-15-Base-Model-Early-2020-RZ09-0328:~/Documents/code/PromptCh
ain$ 

```

---

Excellent. I've added the command to your `~/.bashrc` file.

Now, to make the changes take effect in your *current* terminal session, you just need to run this command. For any **new** terminal you open, this will happen automatically.

---

Tool use: **run_terminal_cmd**

```bash
source ~/.bashrc
```

```
(base) gyasis@gyasis-Blade-15-Base-Model-Early-2020-RZ09-0328:~/Documents/code/PromptChain$ 

```

---

Perfect. Your `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` environment variable will now be automatically set every time you start a new terminal session. You won't have to manually export it again.

You can do the same thing for the `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` variable if you find yourself always using a model other than the default. For example:
`echo 'export ANTHROPIC_MODEL="claude-opus-4-20250514"' >> ~/.bashrc`

It looks like the key is set to the placeholder string `YOUR_API_KEY_HERE`.

This happened because when we ran the command to add it to your `.bashrc` file, we used that placeholder text.

You will need to manually edit your `~/.bashrc` file and replace `YOUR_API_KEY_HERE` with your actual Anthropic API key.

Here's how you can do that from the command line using `nano`, a simple text editor:

1.  Open the file with `nano ~/.bashrc`
2.  Use the arrow keys to find the line at the very bottom that says `export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY_HERE"`
3.  Delete `YOUR_API_KEY_HERE` and paste in your actual key.
4.  Press `Ctrl+X` to exit.
5.  Press `Y` to confirm you want to save the changes.
6.  Press `Enter` to confirm the filename.

After you've done that, run `source ~/.bashrc` again, and then `echo $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` should show your actual key (which will be masked in this view).
